title: How to Create Natural Looking Lip Filler Results. Lip Filler Injection Tips
filename: How to Create Natural Looking Lip Filler Results. Lip Filler Injection Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: Have you been paying attention to the latest trend of reversing lip filler and then going for the natural look what do injectors need to know about this and what does it mean in your consultation when patients are specifically asking for a natural look but they are part of that d…

segments.text: have you been paying attention to the latest trend of reversing lip filler and then going for the natural look what do injectors need to know about this and what does it mean in your consultation when patients are specifically asking for a natural look but they are part of that demographic of younger women who usually wanted to stand out and actually look treated so back in November 2022 I first predicted that we were going to see an increase in these reversals and it's largely in part to I think the nature of to degree fashion so if you are in a crowd of people who where everyone has their lips done it's actually a bit of a statement to have them undone and to go for something natural because then you're the one who stands out so there's an element of me thinking this is about human psychology and differentiation which is a normal survival Instinct I don't judge anyone for wanting to differentiate themselves but it's a ping-ponging that might be occurring combined with the nature of lip filler which is that lip filler over time I think a lot of them will need reversing because if you repeat treatments over time you lose definition

you try and get the definition back by retreating and you end up with overfilled lips it's almost part of the game until you've experienced it it's quite easy to make the mistakes I think it's quite common around the world for people to retreat lips that probably need reversing but you don't get to do that forever so these two different Dynamics there's a clinical picture which is that you actually have a changing result over time and then you have fashion trends and they for once have met together I think for a lot of people where it's time to have it out to start to get afresh and to try and look more beautiful as opposed to just looking like you're different or standing out to the crowd so many of you may have had the same thought that my producer Layla has had which is that when you actually see these people talking about their lip filler that they still look like they've had a bit of lip filler so this is worth understanding is that really what seems to be going on for a lot of that end of the spectrum is that there's a treatment that goes too far it's then reversed and then it's redone to basically a more normal level

but it's still absolutely a basically to the trained eye it's just lip filler to the untrained eye they actually look like what they probably should have looked like all the way along so what are you going to say to your patients when they come in they often ask for things in particular I've heard the term hydrating lips or a hydration type treatments now what's actually going on here I had a patient inbox me not so long ago thinking that this was not dermal filler it was something else and I think a lot of patients are led to believe this certainly at the marketing phase probably not in the consultation that they're booking for something that isn't dermal filler and the reality is most hydrating treatments are just very similar procedures done usually with products that have a lower G Prime so they're more they're less about shape and usually with a bit less of volume placed in but in every other way they're extremely similar the result is just more gentle and you don't have the changes in shape and it just looks like you've drunk four glasses of water better hydrated and but in many other ways it's a very similar approach

and many so-called hydrated treatments just look like filler if you putting in 0.8 of a mil just looks like you've had filler so worth understanding what that what is going on underneath that is what we call a communication Innovation it's when you take the same thing and describe it in a different way and it makes sense to people on a different level but it is still the same thing so hydrating treatment for me fits into that category you're having liptula it's a more appropriate volume and you call it something else so that patients get it quickly and they know that you're not going to make them look like a duck so first thing is when a patient says they want natural there's more to it that you need to unpack in the consultation this is the same as for any request it's well worth spending the time getting them to articulate what it is that they are seeing in their own mind so this can be done with pictures of celebrities I know some people hate that but for me it's very hard to convey in words exactly what you're picturing and a picture of someone who looks similar is helpful so they might show you something that

you've already done if you've already put before and afters out some patients will bring a specific image of it's best when it's one of yours but it could be someone else's before and after and then of course just getting them to describe what they are hoping to see using their own face so you get them to look in the mirror and show that you using their lips what they would like to see and this is just it's almost like a game that you play to make sure it's all said out loud and then you actually use that information to shape their expectations so um or reflect back what they've said and say that you can do it but it's a little process That's essential even if you kind of know before they even speak what you're going to do and what's what is what natural will look like you have to go through that process and every now and then you will find someone who's picturing something that's not attainable or that although they're using the word natural what they're actually asking for is changes in the shape and the projection proportion that you may not always associate with asking for natural so don't always get hung up on the one word get them to say exactly what they what

they're picturing and then design a treatment plan around the details rather than just the term natural now of course there will be many people and there are whole businesses that are built around people who are actually afraid of having their lips treated but really want them treated and those patients often want something more but they start out with something incredibly subtle and I think that's a an acceptable Journey which is you start with something that's so imperceptible that not even their best friend will notice but they at least get a feeling For What It's Like and then they can decide later on they want to be a little bit more adventurous so there will be a cohort of patients who are coming using the term natural as code word for I'm really just scared of getting a bad result but underneath that they actually want a more projected top lip and once you've got to the detail you can actually design around what they actually are saying to you now if you get to the bottom all of this and someone really does just want a very subtle hydrating type treatment little bits of volume no extra shape just want fullness maybe some slight softening of some of the lines then you can go down

the route of Designing a plan directly for that the type of thing that you might do differently for example would be to use a lower G Prime product so we use products that are much softer rather than the higher G Prime product that you might use if you were trying to attain some sort of shape so this might be a what is often used in other parts of the face so fine line fillers for example if you might use deoxane's red density 2 or juvenile Ultra II volatero balance I think it is I don't use all these different products but they are on that softer end of the spectrum they're not for holding shape that's the kind of product that you might go for you might also use a cannula instead of a needle because you just want to add a little bit of volume it's or the hydration as it's sometimes called but you're not trying to create shape and details so it could lend itself better towards a cannula type treatment and finally it could also just be about the amount that you use so you're not going to use a two mils of something if you want to create a really subtle result are you so it's typically going to be kind of 0.3 mils 0.5 mil range if

not even less than that to be honest where you're just creating a difference that the patient knows is real but even their own mum or their sister or their partner will not spot it and that's often the first experience of people who are afraid of having lip fillers want something natural some people will tick along like that for years and others will get that bump in that direction realize that having lips really makes them feel amazing and wants something a bit stronger the next time and it's your job as a clinician to guide that process so how do you avoid the overfilled look so particularly with patients who come in asking for natural or they're already are afraid of over treatment it's really important to lay out some groundwork in terms of the term overfilled and how to avoid that experience probably one of the most common errors is not to emphasize enough the swelling process because swelling especially on someone who's afraid of being over treated can jolt them into state of anxiety and then it changes the perception of the treatment after that no matter what it looks like anyone who's having a treatment who's wanting a natural result you must tell them that swelling is

going to change what they see because in the first 24 hours it can make them think that they've got the wrong thing so you must be sure before you inject them because reassurance after the fact is a lot harder than giving them the forewarning so forewarned is forearmed as they always say tell them it's going to be swollen it may look over treated but it will settle because you can get that unfortunately even if you just do one horizontal injection each side the swelling sometimes becomes too noticeable for some people the next thing is when in doubt put Less in so that you can put more in later so it's way better to have a reason to add more than it is to have a reason to try and take some out that's a much Messier situation so when your patient is anxious and particularly afraid of this you want to be validating your intervention with them so I quite like this technique in fact I've had it done with me and I appreciated it when I had my teeth done is the dentist when she was doing some of the composite bonding she got me to validate that I liked what I was seeing and that's exactly what I

do with patients so I will do a small injection with someone who's particularly anxious allow them to validate it and really I know that it's okay but I want to bring them with me on the journey I need because as their mind perceives the changes they accept them it delays the anxiety and it stops you getting into a situation where the perception is too far the other way and they have anxiety around being over treated because they're swollen and you haven't given them chance to ease into what they see so the rev the revealing of a treatment for someone who's anxious about over treatment is definitely a more sensitive moment than other parts of the consultation and treatment so we want to do it with that reminder that some of this is swelling some of the redness is just from the needle and not to show them in harsh lighting or too close up so they can see particularly the whole face so I like to show my anxious patients a lip treatment where they see their whole face rather than a close-up of just their lips because it gives it more context and I've usually explained about the swelling before I've shown them that and got them to acknowledge that they've heard that because that for me is one of

the key moments where patients go One Direction or the other they can suddenly become very anxious if they think it's too swollen and it's quite hard to bring them back so all of that should come together with someone who's been handled delicately treated gently with a small amount of volume and they should build trust with you and become a patient for life that's hopefully the goal so I hope that helps by the way guys I'm doing a free webinar on the 28th of February it's going to teach you everything you need to know to really pitch yourself correctly in terms of lead prices specifically so how do you put your prices up how do you value yourself correctly in the marketplace it's all about not becoming a commodity you're much more than just one mil so click on the link down below to register [Music]
